breakClass: left-join null member access / non-nullable value-column read on a possibly-null DefaultIfEmpty() entity in a server-side projection (EF Core 10 shaper reads SQL NULL into a non-nullable CLR value type â€” decimal/int/DateTime/enum â€” and throws; EF6 silently produced default(T))
why: Paginated grid over purchase-returns. c=PRPayments left join empty for returns with no payment row. The only filter touching c (line 869) is conditional (`Balance==null || ...`) and casts to (decimal?), so it does NOT force c non-null at the default Balance==null. c.Status (enum, non-nullable) + c.PReturnAmount (non-nullable decimal) read unguarded -> EF Core 10 500 listing any unpaid return. Grid over many rows = high hit probability. Legacy identical (line 910).
finalFix:
In PurchaseReturnController.cs GetPurchaseReturn, guard every non-nullable value-type read of the left-joined PRPayment `c` so a NULL entity yields legacy default(0), mirroring EF6. Edit the three projection lines:

Line 888:  `PaymentStatus = c.Status,`
  ->       `PaymentStatus = c != null ? c.Status : 0,`

Line 890:  `c.PReturnAmount,`
  ->       `PReturnAmount = c != null ? c.PReturnAmount : 0,`
  (give it an explicit member name since the conditional expression is no longer a simple member access; downstream consumers reference `o.PReturnAmount` at line 957, which is unchanged)

Line 892:  `BalanceAmt = a.PRGrandTotal - c.PReturnAmount,`
  ->       `BalanceAmt = a.PRGrandTotal - (c != null ? c.PReturnAmount : 0),`

This preserves EF6's exact output (0 for Status/PReturnAmount, and BalanceAmt = PRGrandTotal when no payment exists) and EF Core 10 translates the `c != null ? c.X : 0` into a NULL-tolerant CASE/COALESCE so unpaid returns no longer throw. This matches the team's already-established remedy (e.g. ReceiptController.cs:2088 `g != null ? g.PReturnAmount : 0`). The line-869 `Balance` filter already casts to (decimal?) and is safe; no other change needed.
reasoning:
CONFIRMED â€” genuine server-side EF Core 10 left-join non-nullable value-column read; high severity. I attempted to refute and could not.

(a) SERVER-SIDE: The projection (PurchaseReturnController.cs lines 876-901) is built into `serverQuery`, an IQueryable. There is NO `.ToList()`/`.AsEnumerable()` boundary before it â€” the first materialization is line 913 `var serverRows = serverQuery.ToList();`. The server-paging fast path (lines 907-912) even calls `serverQuery.Count()` (909) and Skip/Take, so the full tree (including the c.* reads) is translated to SQL. The later `serverRows.Select(o => ...)` (line 937+) is client-side and merely re-projects already-materialized values, so it is not the issue. The flagged members are translated and read by the SQL shaper. Server-side confirmed.

(b) `c` IS GENUINELY NULL-ABLE: lines 851-852 `join c in db.PRPayments ... into pay from c in pay.DefaultIfEmpty()` is a LEFT JOIN. A purchase return with no PRPayment row (an unpaid return â€” a routine state) yields c = NULL. The only filter touching c is line 869, gated by `(Balance == null)` (the default when no balance filter is supplied) and, in its active branches, casts to `(decimal?)c.PReturnAmount`, i.e. a nullable comparison that tolerates SQL NULL. It therefore does NOT force c non-null; unpaid returns reach the projection with c = NULL.

(c) NON-NULLABLE VALUE READS ON NULL ENTITY: Verified entity types in Models/PurchaseReturn.cs: `PRPayment.Status` is `public int Status` (line 153 â€” a plain int, not an enum as the claim said) and `PRPayment.PReturnAmount` is `public decimal PReturnAmount` (line 147). The projection reads `PaymentStatus = c.Status` (888), `c.PReturnAmount` (890) and `BalanceAmt = a.PRGrandTotal - c.PReturnAmount` (892) unguarded. When c is NULL, EF Core 10's shaper materializes SQL NULL into non-nullable CLR int/decimal and throws InvalidOperationException -> HTTP 500. EF6 silently produced default(T)=0.

(d) PORT REGRESSION: Legacy _archive\legacy-mvc5\Controllers\PurchaseReturnController.cs is structurally identical â€” line 867 `from c in pay.DefaultIfEmpty()`, line 906 `PaymentStatus = c.Status`, line 908 `c.PReturnAmount`, line 910 `BalanceAmt = a.PRGrandTotal - c.PReturnAmount`; legacy PRPayment entity types are identical. It worked under EF6, so this is a pure port regression.

(e) CORROBORATION: The same team has already applied the exact `x != null ? x.Field : 0` guard for this precise class across 6+ controllers, INCLUDING the identical `PReturnAmount`-from-left-joined-PRPayment case at ReceiptController.cs:2088 (`PReturnAmount = g != null ? g.PReturnAmount : 0`) and an explicit comment at PurchaseEntryController.cs:8005 ("s is left-joined (DefaultIfEmpty) -> cast non-nullable struct columns to nullable"). This controller's grid simply wasn't fixed yet.

SEVERITY = HIGH: this is the default paginated purchase-return grid with no balance filter (Balance == null path active). Any unpaid return (common, realistic) is listed and makes c = NULL; a grid over many rows has high probability of hitting one -> 500.

Note correcting the claim: c.Status is `int`, not an enum, so the default is 0 (default(int)) â€” the proposed `default` still yields 0, so the fix is output-correct.
